title: Phyllocephalum tenue
taxonomy: Phyllocephalum tenue is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Phyllocephalum. Phyllocephalum tenue is native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India. The exact classification details may vary depending on the source, but it is widely recognized as a member of the aster family. This classification is based on its characteristic flowers and growth habit.
uses: Phyllocephalum tenue is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in India and other parts of Asia. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to manage conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy for these purposes. Additionally, Phyllocephalum tenue is often used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
